name: Herbalisti reference lane coverage
policy: Jurisdiction-lane coverage and rights-review status for reference discovery. This export contains metadata and governance notes only; it does not include copied book text, treatment protocols, or personalized advice.

lanes.label: US
lanes.message: US archive lane active with rights-cleared public-domain and permissively reusable source records.

lanes.label: UK
lanes.message: UK archive lane active with rights-cleared public-domain and permissively reusable source records.

lanes.label: Australia
lanes.message: Australia lane is prepared for rights-cleared archive intake; no Australian reference books are published yet.

australiaQueue.allowedContentRule: Book-like sources only, with full text allowed only when public domain, CC0, CC BY, CC BY-SA, or similarly permissive item-level reuse rights are clear.
australiaQueue.excludedContentRule: No newspapers, blogs, general web pages, serial feeds, uncertain-rights scans, or platform-only records enter the corpus.
australiaQueue.culturalSafety: First Australian and Indigenous knowledge remains restricted metadata only unless explicit rights, authority, and culturally safe review are obtained.

candidateSources.name: Trove / National Library of Australia
candidateSources.permittedUseAtThisStage: Manual discovery notes and item-level rights review only.
candidateSources.rightsBoundary: Trove API terms cover metadata access. Digital objects and full text require item-level open licence, public-domain certainty, or content-owner permission.
candidateSources.noIngestReason: API use requires an active key, and harvesting full text or using records for AI modelling can require higher review. This lane does not depend on a Trove key.
candidateSources.nextAction: Use manual catalogue search now; consider an API key later only for metadata review, not full-text ingestion.

candidateSources.name: State Library Victoria catalogue and digitised books
candidateSources.permittedUseAtThisStage: Manual candidate discovery and catalogue note taking only.
candidateSources.rightsBoundary: Only item-level public-domain, CC0, CC BY, or similarly permissive text can move into the corpus.
candidateSources.noIngestReason: State-library discovery pages can mix public-domain metadata, digitised objects, and third-party rights. Each book needs its own rights review.
candidateSources.nextAction: Manually identify book-level candidates and record item-level copyright/licence evidence.

candidateSources.name: State Library of New South Wales catalogue and digital collections
candidateSources.permittedUseAtThisStage: Manual candidate discovery and catalogue note taking only.
candidateSources.rightsBoundary: Only item-level public-domain, CC0, CC BY, or similarly permissive text can move into the corpus.
candidateSources.noIngestReason: Catalogue records and digital objects can carry different reuse terms. This lane requires a separate rights proof before acquisition.
candidateSources.nextAction: Manually screen book-level results and preserve source-page rights evidence with each candidate.

candidateSources.name: State Library of Queensland catalogue and digital collections
candidateSources.permittedUseAtThisStage: Manual candidate discovery and catalogue note taking only.
candidateSources.rightsBoundary: Only item-level public-domain, CC0, CC BY, or similarly permissive text can move into the corpus.
candidateSources.noIngestReason: Digital collection entries need item-level copyright checks before any local text acquisition.
candidateSources.nextAction: Manually screen catalogue records for book-like, rights-clear, full-text candidates.

candidateSources.name: Biodiversity Heritage Library Australia-linked holdings
candidateSources.permittedUseAtThisStage: Manual candidate discovery for botanical books with medicinal-plant relevance.
candidateSources.rightsBoundary: BHL items can carry title-level rights statements. Only public-domain or permissively licensed full text can move into the corpus.
candidateSources.noIngestReason: Many botanical works are taxonomic rather than practical medicine; candidates need both relevance and rights review.
candidateSources.nextAction: Use this as a botanical backfill lane only after practical health relevance is confirmed.
